title: Learn a historical out-lap warm-up tax instead of fixed undercut rules
summary: Official F1 and Pirelli sources emphasize that overcuts happen when fresh tyres, especially harder compounds or cold-track cases, are slow to switch on.
rationale: Official F1 and Pirelli sources emphasize that overcuts happen when fresh tyres, especially harder compounds or cold-track cases, are slow to switch on. A learned warm-up penalty should be more faithful than static finishability heuristics.
implementation_hint: From training races only, collect laps immediately after `PitStop=1` and estimate OOF warm-up penalties by race/year/compound/stint phase versus same-race baselines. Merge shrinkage-smoothed features like `FreshHard_OutlapTax`, `PitNow_ExpectedGainMinusWarmupTax`, and `OvercutFriendlyRegime` back into the row model.
expected_effect: Cuts false positives on old-tyre laps where pitting now is strategically bad because the out-lap is likely weak.
risk: Sparse post-stop samples for wet tyres or rare circuits; needs hierarchical smoothing to avoid noisy priors.
